No Poverty.reason: Geodetic surveying supports land management and disaster monitoring, aligning with Target 1.4 (land rights) and Target 1.5 (resilience to disasters).

Zero Hunger.reason: The course's focus on GPS and GNSS principles provides the technological basis for precision agriculture, supporting Target 2.4 on resilient agricultural practices.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course's application in environmental monitoring can contribute to tracking pollution, which is relevant to Target 3.9 (reduce deaths from pollution).

Quality Education.reason: As a university course in a specialized technical field, it directly provides quality tertiary education and relevant skills, fulfilling Targets 4.3 and 4.4.
evidence: develop students’ ability to apply modern measurement techniques

Gender Equality.reason: No evidence found in the course syllabus to link to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The course's focus on environmental monitoring and gravity field models provides tools to monitor water resources, supporting Target 6.6.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: Geodetic applications in engineering are crucial for planning and constructing energy infrastructure, including renewables, supporting Targets 7.a and 7.b.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops skills in modern measurement techniques and explores future trends, directly contributing to Target 8.2 on technological upgrading and innovation.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course is foundational for developing sustainable and resilient infrastructure (Target 9.1) and enhances scientific research and technological capabilities (Target 9.5).
evidence: providing the theoretical and technical foundation for surveying

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No evidence found in the course syllabus to link to reduced inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Content on engineering surveying and crustal movement monitoring is essential for sustainable urban planning (Target 11.3) and disaster resilience (Target 11.5).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The application of geodesy in environmental monitoring supports the sustainable management and efficient use of natural resources, as mentioned in Target 12.2.

Climate Action.reason: Monitoring Earth dynamic phenomena like tides is crucial for understanding climate change impacts and strengthening resilience to climate-related hazards (Target 13.1).

Life Below Water.reason: The course includes ocean monitoring and the study of tides and geostrophic currents, directly contributing to scientific knowledge for managing marine ecosystems (Targets 14.2, 14.a).
evidence: Tides, Polar Motion and Geostrophic Current

Life on Land.reason: Satellite measurement techniques and environmental monitoring are used to monitor deforestation and land degradation, supporting conservation of terrestrial ecosystems (Targets 15.1, 15.3).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Land surveying and coordinate systems provide the technical basis for property registration systems, foundational for the rule of law (Target 16.3).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course relies on global systems like GPS/GNSS and global gravity field models, reflecting international scientific cooperation and data sharing (Targets 17.6, 17.18).
